title: Pod Lifecycle States
description:
## What does the `CrashLoopBackOff` status mean for a Pod?

You observe the following when running `kubectl get pods`:

```
NAME          READY   STATUS             RESTARTS   AGE
my-app-xyz    0/1     CrashLoopBackOff   5          3m
```

What is the Kubernetes control plane communicating with this status?

options.text: The pod image could not be pulled from the container registry
The container starts, crashes, and Kubernetes keeps restarting it with exponential backoff delay
The pod is waiting for a PersistentVolume to become available
The pod has been evicted from the node due to resource pressure

explanation: `CrashLoopBackOff` means the container is repeatedly crashing after startup. Kubernetes restarts it automatically but introduces increasing delays (backoff) between attempts to avoid overwhelming the system. Common causes include a bad entrypoint command, missing environment variables, or application errors on startup.

hints.title: Interpreting pod status
hints.body: Use `kubectl describe pod <name>` to see the Events section — it shows exactly why the container is failing.

hints.title: Reading container logs
hints.body: Even a crashed container leaves logs behind. Use `--previous` to read the logs from the last crash.
